instruction: What did Hamza build with Azuha?
output: Built a GPT-style large language model completely from scratch following the book Build a Large Language Model From Scratch by Sebastian Raschka. Implemented tokenization, multi-head attention, transformer blocks, pretraining, and instruction fine-tuning with LoRA. The model is named Azuha and serves as Hamza's personal assistant.

LoRA stands for Low-Rank Adaptation. Instead of training all model weights, LoRA freezes the pretrained weights and adds small trainable matrices A and B alongside the attention layers. Only those small matrices are trained, which makes fine-tuning much faster and cheaper. I was fine-tuned using LoRA with rank 4.
